Protocol_Name: rTMS 10 Hz Left DLPFC for MDD (Standard)
Evidence_Summary: Guideline-supported by APA, CANMAT (2023), NICE. FDA-cleared protocol basis. Multiple meta-analyses confirm efficacy vs sham.
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Figure-8 coil at F3 (10-20 system) or MRI-guided DLPFC
Monitoring_Requirements: PHQ-9 weekly; suicide risk assessment; adverse event log
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: If <25% PHQ-9 improvement after 15 sessions: clinician review for parameter adjustment or discontinuation
Notes: Standard FDA-cleared protocol. 10 Hz most studied frequency. Motor threshold calibration required each session.

Protocol_Name: iTBS Left DLPFC for MDD
Evidence_Summary: Non-inferior to 10 Hz rTMS per THREE-D trial (Blumberger 2018, Lancet, n=388). CANMAT 2023 supports. Major advantage: ~3 min vs 37.5 min session.
Intensity: 80% active motor threshold (THREE-D protocol) or 120% RMT
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Figure-8 coil at F3 or MRI-guided DLPFC
Monitoring_Requirements: PHQ-9 weekly; suicide risk assessment; adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Same as standard rTMS; slightly shorter exposure
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Same escalation rules as PRO-001
Notes: THREE-D trial: response 49% vs 47% (10Hz); remission 32% vs 27%. Shorter sessions = higher throughput.

Protocol_Name: tDCS Left DLPFC for MDD
On_Label_vs_Off_Label: On-label (Flow FL-100 only for MDD; off-label for other tDCS devices)
Evidence_Summary: Literature-supported. 2024 meta-analysis: SMD=-0.355 vs sham. Flow FL-100 PMA-approved Dec 2025 for moderate-severe MDD (not treatment-refractory).
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Anode at F3; Cathode at Fp2 or right DLPFC
Monitoring_Requirements: PHQ-9 weekly; skin inspection at electrode sites; adverse event log
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: If no response after 10 sessions: clinician review
Notes: 2 mA significantly better than 1 mA. Flow FL-100 is ONLY FDA-approved tDCS device. Other tDCS devices are off-label.

Protocol_Name: CES for Depression (Adjunctive)
On_Label_vs_Off_Label: Off-label for depression in US (Alpha-Stim cleared for anxiety/insomnia only)
Evidence_Summary: Emerging for primary MDD. Cochrane 2014: insufficient evidence. Cambridge meta-analysis: g=0.654 but mixed quality. Better evidence for anxiety-predominant presentations.
Monitoring_Requirements: PHQ-9 weekly; GAD-7 (anxiety); sleep diary
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: If no response after 2 weeks: clinician review
Notes: Alpha-Stim NOT FDA-cleared for depression in US. Depression indication available outside US. Better evidence for anxiety comorbidity.

Protocol_Name: taVNS for Depression (Emerging)
Evidence_Summary: Emerging. 2023 meta-analysis: significant HAMD improvement but GRADE 'low to very low'. Small heterogeneous trials.
Target_Region: Auricular branch of vagus nerve (cymba concha)
Intensity: 0.1-5 mA (subsensory to perceptible); pulse width 200-500 µs
Monitoring_Requirements: PHQ-9 weekly; cardiac monitoring if indicated; adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Ear discomfort; skin irritation; cough reflex; dizziness
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician-guided parameter adjustment only
Notes: No FDA clearance for depression. Evidence graded 'low to very low' by GRADE. Research protocol only.

Protocol_Name: Neurofeedback Alpha Asymmetry for Depression (Draft)
Evidence_Summary: Emerging with methodological limitations. 2025 review: d=0.38-1.50 but RCTs with blinded assessment show attenuated effects. Placebo response concern.
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: EEG electrodes at F3, F4, reference
Monitoring_Requirements: PHQ-9 weekly; qEEG progress tracking; engagement monitoring
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Protocol adjustment based on qEEG progress every 10 sessions
Notes: Blinding concerns: RCTs with sham show attenuated effects. Present as clinician-guided adjunctive only.

Protocol_Name: Deep TMS H7-Coil for OCD (FDA-Cleared Protocol)
Evidence_Summary: FDA De Novo cleared Aug 2018. Pivotal RCT (Carmi 2019): 38.1% response vs 11.1% sham. Symptom provocation + dTMS.
Monitoring_Requirements: Y-BOCS every 2 weeks; PHQ-9 (comorbid depression); adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Headache; transient anxiety increase during provocation; jaw pain
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Continue to 40 sessions if partial response at 30 sessions (post-hoc analysis supports continued improvement)
Notes: H7-coil targets ACC/mPFC, NOT DLPFC. Symptom provocation is integral. First non-invasive device cleared for OCD.

Protocol_Name: rTMS 1 Hz Bilateral SMA for OCD (Off-Label)
Evidence_Summary: Literature-supported. Meta-analyses show SMA 1 Hz effective for OCD. Double-blind RCT: significant Y-BOCS decrease at 6 weeks, sustained at follow-up.
Monitoring_Requirements: Y-BOCS every 2 weeks; PHQ-9; adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Scalp discomfort; occasional twitching during SMA stimulation
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review after 15 sessions for response assessment
Notes: Off-label protocol. SMA/pre-SMA is primary target. Improvements in compulsions more than obsessions in some studies.

Protocol_Name: CES for Anxiety (Alpha-Stim)
Evidence_Summary: Literature-supported. 2022 meta-analysis: g=-0.625 overall; g=-1.218 primary anxiety. Alpha-Stim: ES=-1.05 (p<0.0001).
Monitoring_Requirements: GAD-7 weekly; PHQ-9 (comorbid depression); adverse event log
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review after 2 weeks; adjust treatment duration based on response
Notes: Alpha-Stim FDA-cleared for anxiety. Strongest CES evidence is for anxiety.

Protocol_Name: CES for Insomnia (Alpha-Stim)
Evidence_Summary: Emerging-Literature-supported. CES significantly improved insomnia in anxiety-predominant patients (ES=-1.02). Half of high-quality studies show improvement.
Monitoring_Requirements: ISI weekly; sleep diary; PHQ-9; GAD-7
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review after 2 weeks
Notes: Alpha-Stim cleared for insomnia. Evidence stronger when anxiety is comorbid. Differentiate primary vs secondary insomnia.

Protocol_Name: rTMS for PTSD (Right DLPFC)
Evidence_Summary: Literature-supported. 2025 meta-analysis: SMD=1.29 (p<0.0001) for PTSD symptoms. Dose-response: more sessions = greater improvement.
Monitoring_Requirements: PCL-5 weekly; PHQ-9; safety screening; trauma-related distress monitoring
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Trauma-related anxiety in clinical setting; headache; scalp discomfort
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review after 10 sessions; consider extending if partial response
Notes: No FDA clearance for PTSD. Both HF and LF protocols studied; no clear superiority. Support during sessions recommended for PTSD patients.

Protocol_Name: rTMS M1 for Chronic Pain / Fibromyalgia
Evidence_Summary: Literature-supported. European IFCN 2020: Level B recommendation. HF-rTMS M1 significantly decreased VAS scores in fibromyalgia. Effects may not be permanent.
Target_Region: Primary Motor Cortex (M1) contralateral to pain
Total_Course: 5-10 sessions; maintenance sessions may be needed
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Figure-8 coil over M1 hand area contralateral to pain
Monitoring_Requirements: VAS/NRS pain pre/post session; BPI; PHQ-9; adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Headache; scalp discomfort; temporary pain increase
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: If response noted: consider maintenance schedule (weekly or biweekly sessions)
Notes: IFCN Level B for fibromyalgia. M1 targeting for sensory component. DLPFC for affective pain component.

Protocol_Name: tDCS M1 for Chronic Pain / Fibromyalgia
Evidence_Summary: Literature-supported. European IFCN 2017: Level B for fibromyalgia. Home-based tDCS meta-analysis: SMD=-0.95 for chronic pain.
Target_Region: Primary Motor Cortex (M1) anode; contralateral supraorbital cathode
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Anode over M1 contralateral to pain; Cathode contralateral supraorbital
Monitoring_Requirements: VAS/NRS pain pre/post session; BPI; skin inspection; adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Skin irritation; tingling; headache; burn risk with poor electrode prep
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review after 10 sessions; consider home-based continuation if response noted
Notes: Level B recommendation for fibromyalgia. Home-based protocols studied with good adherence.

Protocol_Name: sTMS for Acute Migraine (eNeura)
Evidence_Summary: FDA-cleared for acute and preventive migraine. Pivotal RCT: 38% pain-free at 2h vs 17% sham.
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Toroidal coil placed at back of head by patient
Monitoring_Requirements: Headache diary; attack frequency; MIDAS/HIT-6 periodic
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Lightheadedness (mild); very low seizure risk (single pulse)
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: N/A — as-needed acute treatment
Notes: Self-administered at home. Single-pulse = very low seizure risk. Prescription required.

Protocol_Name: gammaCore nVNS for Cluster Headache / Migraine
On_Label_vs_Off_Label: On-label (gammaCore cleared for CH and migraine)
Evidence_Summary: FDA-cleared for episodic CH (acute + prevention) and migraine (acute + prevention). PRESTO trial: pain freedom superior to sham.
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Gel-coated contact surface applied to neck over vagus nerve
Monitoring_Requirements: Headache diary; attack frequency; adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Neck twitching; voice changes; cough; bradycardia (rare)
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: N/A — as-needed use with prescribed dose limits
Notes: Cervical nVNS, not auricular. Self-administered. NOT for chronic cluster headache.

Protocol_Name: VNS Therapy for Drug-Resistant Epilepsy
Evidence_Summary: Guideline-supported. FDA PMA-approved 1997. 60% seizure reduction at 3 years (CORE-VNS). >125,000 implanted worldwide.
Intensity: 0.25-3.5 mA (ramped up over weeks); pulse width 250-500 µs
Session_Duration: Continuous (30s ON / 5 min OFF duty cycle)
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Helical bipolar electrode on left vagus nerve; subcutaneous IPG in chest
Monitoring_Requirements: Seizure diary; AED level monitoring; device interrogation; voice/swallowing assessment
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Hoarseness; cough; dyspnea; throat discomfort; surgical site infection
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Gradual current increase (0.25 mA steps) over weeks to therapeutic level; AutoStim for seizure-triggered stimulation
Notes: Surgical implant. Progressive benefit over time. Not a cure but reduces seizure frequency.

Protocol_Name: ANT-DBS for Drug-Resistant Epilepsy
Evidence_Summary: FDA-approved 2018 (SANTE trial). 68% responder rate (≥50% reduction) at 5 years.
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: DBS leads in bilateral ANT; subcutaneous IPG in chest
Monitoring_Requirements: Seizure diary; device interrogation; psychiatric monitoring (depression risk); MRI conditional review
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Memory impairment (15%); depressive mood (13%); surgical risks (hemorrhage 1.8%, infection 4.5%)
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Voltage titration over weeks; programming adjustments based on seizure response
Notes: SANTE trial basis. Psychiatric monitoring important (15% memory, 13% depression). SUDEP case reported in MORE registry.

Protocol_Name: DBS STN/GPi for Parkinson's Disease
Evidence_Summary: Guideline-supported (AAN). 30+ years evidence. 40-60% UPDRS motor improvement. Adaptive DBS (BrainSense) FDA approved Feb 2025.
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: DBS leads in bilateral STN or GPi; subcutaneous IPG
Monitoring_Requirements: UPDRS Part III; medication diary; quality of life; psychiatric screening; device interrogation
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Dysarthria; dyskinesia; mood changes; impulse control (STN); surgical risks
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Stimulation parameter optimization over months; medication adjustment post-surgery
Notes: Standard of care for advanced PD. STN: more medication reduction; GPi: less dyskinesia/mood risk.

Protocol_Name: DBS VIM for Essential Tremor
Evidence_Summary: Guideline-supported. FDA-approved 1997. 40-80% tremor reduction in long-term follow-up.
Monitoring_Requirements: Tremor rating scale; functional assessment; speech evaluation (bilateral risk); device interrogation
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Paresthesias; dysarthria (bilateral risk); gait instability; battery replacement
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Stimulation parameter optimization; consider cycling to reduce tolerance
Notes: Alternative: MRgFUS thalamotomy (lesional, irreversible but no hardware). Bilateral DBS increases dysarthria risk.

Protocol_Name: DBS GPi for Dystonia
Evidence_Summary: Guideline-supported for drug-refractory primary dystonia. HDE H020007 (2003) → PMA (2025).
Total_Course: Permanent; benefit may take weeks-months to develop
Monitoring_Requirements: Burke-Fahn-Marsden scale; quality of life; device interrogation
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Surgical risks; dysarthria; gait changes; mood effects (rare with GPi)
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Gradual parameter optimization; benefit develops over weeks-months (unlike PD/ET where response is rapid)
Notes: Response develops slowly over weeks-months (unlike PD). Genetic testing may predict response (DYT1+).

Protocol_Name: Deep TMS H4-Coil for Smoking Cessation
Evidence_Summary: FDA-cleared 2020. First TMS device cleared for any addiction indication. H4-coil targets bilateral insula/PFC.
Total_Course: 6 weeks (with brief smoking cue exposure before each session)
Monitoring_Requirements: Smoking status (exhaled CO); craving VAS; Fagerström; adverse event log
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Combined with behavioral counseling and/or NRT for best outcomes
Notes: H4-coil different from H7 (OCD) and H1 (depression). Smoking cue exposure protocol integral.

Protocol_Name: taVNS for Opioid Withdrawal (Sparrow)
On_Label_vs_Off_Label: On-label (Sparrow Ascent cleared for opioid withdrawal)
Evidence_Summary: FDA-cleared. Transcutaneous auricular neurostimulation for opioid withdrawal symptom relief. Used with standard symptomatic medications.
Monitoring_Requirements: COWS; SOWS; symptom tracking; adverse event log
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Used in conjunction with standard withdrawal management
Notes: Only FDA-cleared taVNS device. Specifically for opioid withdrawal, NOT psychiatric indications.

Protocol_Name: rTMS for Post-Stroke Motor Recovery (Inhibitory Contralesional)
Evidence_Summary: Literature-supported. 2024 meta-analysis: significant FMA-UE improvement. Best evidence for subacute phase. Both inhibitory contralesional and excitatory ipsilesional approaches effective.
Monitoring_Requirements: FMA-UE; Barthel Index; modified Rankin; adverse event log
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review after 10 sessions; consider ipsilesional excitatory if contralesional insufficient
Notes: Inhibitory contralesional best for less-severe hemiplegia in acute/subacute. Excitatory ipsilesional for severe/chronic.

Protocol_Name: rTMS for Post-Stroke Motor Recovery (Excitatory Ipsilesional)
Evidence_Summary: Literature-supported. Excitatory ipsilesional protocol for severe hemiplegia in chronic phase.
Monitoring_Requirements: FMA-UE; grip strength; ARAT; adverse event log
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review after 10 sessions
Notes: Better for severe hemiplegia in chronic phase. Combined with task-specific therapy for best results.

Protocol_Name: Neurofeedback Theta/Beta for ADHD (Clinician Draft — Evidence Caution)
Evidence_Summary: CRITICAL: NOT supported by blinded outcomes. Cortese 2024 (JAMA Psychiatry, n=2472): SMD=0.04 (probably blinded). Effects seen ONLY with unblinded parent-rated measures. Standard protocols show limited signal on blinded outcomes.
Monitoring_Requirements: ASRS or Conners/SNAP; BRIEF; CPT; qEEG progress; engagement tracking
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Minimal adverse events; primary concern is opportunity cost vs evidence-based treatments
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: qEEG review every 10 sessions; clinician review of blinded outcome measures
Notes: EVIDENCE CAUTION: SMD=0.04 on blinded outcomes (Cortese 2024). Do NOT present as established treatment. Opportunity cost concern. Inform patients of evidence limitations.

Protocol_Name: rTMS 1 Hz Temporal Cortex for Tinnitus
Evidence_Summary: Emerging. Modest short-term benefit (THI, VAS improvement). NOT durable at 6 months. Laterality affects response.
Monitoring_Requirements: THI; TFI; VAS loudness/distress; audiogram at baseline
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Headache; scalp discomfort; temporary tinnitus loudness increase
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: If no response after 10 sessions: consider combined temporal + prefrontal protocol
Notes: Short-term benefit only. Not durable at 6 months. Left-sided and bilateral tinnitus respond better than right-sided.

Protocol_Name: rTMS for Post-TBI Cognitive Enhancement (Emerging)
Evidence_Summary: Emerging. Small studies and case reports suggest improvement in attention and processing speed. No RCT-level evidence.
Monitoring_Requirements: MoCA; Trail Making Test; BRIEF-A; PCL-5 if trauma-related; adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Seizure risk (post-TBI); headache; check for skull defects/metallic implants from surgical repair
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review after 10 sessions
Notes: No RCT-level evidence specifically for TBI cognitive enhancement. Screen carefully for post-surgical metallic implants.

Protocol_Name: VNS Therapy for Treatment-Resistant Depression
Evidence_Summary: FDA PMA-approved Jul 2005 for TRD (≥4 adequate Rx failures). Adjunctive long-term treatment.
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Helical bipolar electrode on left vagus nerve; subcutaneous IPG
Monitoring_Requirements: MADRS or HAM-D monthly; suicide risk assessment; quality of life; device interrogation
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Gradual current increase over weeks; response may take 3-12 months — do not discontinue early
Notes: Response takes months — not weeks. Specifically for ≥4 Rx failures. Surgical implant required.

Protocol_Name: DBS ALIC for Severe OCD (HDE)
Evidence_Summary: HDE-approved 2009 for severe treatment-resistant OCD. Failed ≥3 SSRIs required. Reclaim DBS. Growing evidence base.
Monitoring_Requirements: Y-BOCS monthly; CGI; depression screening; psychiatric monitoring; device interrogation
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Surgical risks; hypomania risk; mood instability; weight changes
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Gradual parameter optimization by experienced DBS team; psychiatric monitoring mandatory
Notes: HDE = humanitarian device. Requires institutional oversight. Failed ≥3 SSRIs. Only for severe, refractory OCD.

Protocol_Name: rTMS for Parkinson's Disease (Adjunctive)
Evidence_Summary: Literature-supported as adjunct. 2015 JAMA Neurology meta-analysis: significant motor improvement. 2026 meta-analysis: improved UPDRS-III, FOG-Q, MoCA, BDI.
Target_Region: M1 or DLPFC (depending on target symptoms)
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Figure-8 coil over M1 (motor symptoms) or DLPFC (depression/cognition)
Monitoring_Requirements: UPDRS Part III; MoCA; PHQ-9; adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: DBS presence is contraindication to TMS; seizure risk; headache
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review based on UPDRS response
Notes: Adjunctive only. DBS is contraindication. Multiple targets depending on symptom profile.

Protocol_Name: rTMS Bilateral Sequential for Anxious Depression
Evidence_Summary: Literature-supported. Retrospective cohort: both unilateral and bilateral produce ~44-45% GAD-7 improvement. Sequential bilateral may address both depression and anxiety.
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Figure-8 coil at F3 then F4
Monitoring_Requirements: PHQ-9; GAD-7; adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Longer session duration; headache; scalp discomfort
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review after 15 sessions
Notes: For comorbid anxiety-depression presentations. Bilateral sequential addresses both circuits.

Protocol_Name: HRV Biofeedback for Stress and Autonomic Regulation
Evidence_Summary: Literature-supported for stress/anxiety reduction. Often used as adjunctive to primary neuromodulation protocol.
Target_Region: Autonomic nervous system via HRV monitoring
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Heart rate sensor (chest strap or pulse oximeter)
Monitoring_Requirements: HRV metrics; GAD-7; stress diary
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Identify resonance frequency; progressive home practice
Notes: Adjunctive method. Not a primary neuromodulation treatment. Good for autonomic dysregulation component. Source: Lehrer & Gevirtz 2014 meta-analysis; Gevirtz 2013 review. Parameters vary by device.

Protocol_Name: TPS for Parkinson Disease (Emerging evidence adjunct)
Evidence_Summary: Emerging small trials for adjunct motor symptoms with TPS; not guideline-supported. Registry row supports evidence traceability only.
Monitoring_Requirements: UPDRS Part III; MoCA; PHQ-9; adverse event log
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician milestone review per institutional governance
Notes: Synthetic PD+TPS protocol linkage for API tests — not treatment guidance.

Protocol_Name: tACS 10 Hz Left DLPFC for Treatment-Resistant Depression
On_Label_vs_Off_Label: Off-label (investigational; no FDA clearance for tACS)
Evidence_Summary: RCT NCT06812923 (N=52, 4-wk course); 2024 review (PMID 39261427); mechanistic review (PMID 33211157).
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: 4x1-ring HD montage centred F3 or standard 2-electrode F3-contralateral
Monitoring_Requirements: EEG at baseline; scalp inspection each session; phosphene threshold check
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: If no response at 10 sessions: clinician review of frequency/placement
Notes: Draft — tACS TRD; investigational. 15 mA/77.5 Hz alt arm in NCT06812923 non-standard.

Protocol_Name: tACS 40 Hz Gamma DLPFC/PFC for Alzheimer's Disease
Evidence_Summary: Phase 2 pilot NCT06826261 (N=30, 40 Hz, 8 wks, home-based); 2025 narrative review (PMID 40142358) supports gamma entrainment.
Monitoring_Requirements: Remote digital monitoring portal; caregiver-administered; weekly video check
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Scalp warmth/tingling log; pediatric AE protocol (PMID 38761518)
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: If cognitive decline accelerates: pause and clinician review
Notes: Condition updated to CON-021 Alzheimer's. Intensity verify flag.

Protocol_Name: tACS 40 Hz Temporal Lobe for Schizophrenia Auditory Hallucinations
Evidence_Summary: Completed RCT NCT05282329 (N=50, gamma, 5 d/wk x 4 wks, 2 mA, 20 min); completed pilot NCT04545294 (N=36, 6 Hz theta).
Monitoring_Requirements: PANSS/AHRS weekly; EEG at baseline
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Scalp tingling; phosphenes; EPS check (antipsychotic co-use)
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: If hallucinations worsen or new neurological symptoms: stop and refer
Notes: Condition updated to CON-023 Schizophrenia.

Protocol_Name: tACS Theta (6 Hz) Frontoparietal for MCI Working Memory
Evidence_Summary: Ongoing RCT NCT04135742 (N=195, tACS+CCT); largest tACS trial in MCI.
Monitoring_Requirements: EEG at baseline; cognitive testing at 0/post
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Adjust if EEG shows no theta entrainment at session 5
Notes: Condition updated to CON-022 MCI. Multiple verify flags — NCT04545294 omits intensity/duration/sessions.

Protocol_Name: tPBM 823 nm Bilateral DLPFC for MDD (ELATED-2 Protocol)
Evidence_Summary: ELATED-2 pilot RCT (N=21, d=0.90-1.5; PMID 30346890); NCT05573074 Phase 2 N=120. ELATED-3 (PMID 35950904) established low-dose inefficacy threshold.
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Bilateral DLPFC simultaneous; 28.7 x 2 cm² probe
Monitoring_Requirements: HAM-D17 baseline + biweekly; skin temperature; operator eye protection
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: If <25% HAM-D improvement at 8 sessions: clinician review
Notes: NCT05573074 different device (tPBM-2.0, 291.7 mW/cm²) — parameters not interchangeable. FDA 510k IYO/GZE transcranial brain use: verify via accessdata.fda.gov.

Protocol_Name: tPBM 808 nm Forehead for Chronic TBI Cognitive Rehabilitation
Evidence_Summary: NIH-funded review (PMID 29131369); NCT06956404 recruiting N=70 (808 nm, 18 sessions); 2016 LED review (PMID 28001756).
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Forehead (F3/F4/Fz) via flexible optical fiber + custom cap
Monitoring_Requirements: MoCA/neuropsych testing pre/post; scalp temperature; skin inspection
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: If cognitive performance declines mid-point: pause and refer
Notes: Trial recruiting 2024-2026; parameters are trial-arm values only.

Protocol_Name: tPBM 1064 nm Right Forehead for Anxiety Disorders
Evidence_Summary: NCT07133893 (N=280, not yet recruiting, 1064 nm, 8 min); Schiffer 2009 pilot (PMID 19995444, N=10); Maiello 2019 GAD pilot (PMID 31647775).
Monitoring_Requirements: GAD-7/HAM-A baseline + post; no EEG required
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Scalp warmth; mild headache; eye protection mandatory
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: If anxiety worsens at 2 wks: stop and review
Notes: Grade D — weakest evidence. Multiple verify flags for irradiance and session count.

Protocol_Name: tPBM Symbyx Neuro Helmet (635+810 nm) for Parkinson's Disease
Evidence_Summary: Symbyx Neuro NCT06036433; Liebert 2021 (PMID 34215216) proof-of-concept N=12, 1-yr sustained; Liebert 2024 5-yr follow-up (PMID 39385144).
Frequency_Hz: CW + pulsed 635 nm red + 810 nm NIR
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Symbyx Neuro LED helmet with 20 LED clusters
Monitoring_Requirements: MDS-UPDRS-III; functional motor tests; skin inspection
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Scalp warmth; no serious AEs reported
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review per institutional governance
Notes: Symbyx helmet; do NOT substitute for DBS in advanced PD.

Protocol_Name: tACS 77.5 Hz for Chronic Insomnia
Evidence_Summary: Zhu 2023 multisite RCT (PMID 38176353, N=120, 77.5 Hz, 15 mA, 20 sessions x 4 wks); Wang 2019 (PMID 31786573).
Intensity: 15 mA (above standard tES — research protocol only)
Monitoring_Requirements: PSQI/ISI weekly; scalp inspection; AE log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Skin redness; phosphenes; high intensity requires close monitoring
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review at 10 sessions
Notes: SAFETY: 15 mA above standard; 2025 Lee trial negative, 2025 Wang MPC alpha positive — parameter choice must be documented.

Protocol_Name: tACS Personalized Theta for Parkinson's Disease
Evidence_Summary: Del Felice 2019 (PMID 30921609) personalized theta-tACS + PT crossover RCT.
Monitoring_Requirements: UPDRS Part III; EEG; gait assessment
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review per protocol
Notes: Personalised theta-tACS. Combined with physical therapy.

Protocol_Name: VNS Therapy for Post-Stroke Upper-Limb Rehabilitation (Vivistim)
Evidence_Summary: FDA PMA P200051 (Aug 2021); VNS-REHAB pivotal trial (PMID 34115889) paired VNS + rehab 2.7x improvement in FMA-UE vs sham.
Target_Region: Left cervical vagus nerve (implanted) + upper-limb rehab
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Helical bipolar electrode on left vagus; IPG in chest; paired to rehab motion
Monitoring_Requirements: FMA-UE; Barthel; device interrogation
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Current titration per clinician; paired with task-specific rehab
Notes: First FDA-cleared VNS for post-stroke motor rehab. Paired VNS during rehab.

Protocol_Name: SMR Neurofeedback for Drug-Resistant Epilepsy
Evidence_Summary: Tan 2009 (PMID 19027345) meta-analysis SMR NFB for epilepsy; Sterman 2000 (PMID 10858337).
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Standard SMR montage (Cz/C3/C4 with earlobe reference)
Monitoring_Requirements: Seizure diary; AED levels; EEG progress
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Minimal; monitor for seizure frequency change
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review every 10 sessions
Notes: Top DB citation PMID 29034226 is HRV paper — SMR epilepsy evidence per Tan 2009.

Protocol_Name: taVNS Adjunct for Major Depressive Disorder
Evidence_Summary: Rong 2016 (PMID 29593576); 2023 meta-analysis (PMID 37230264) GRADE low to very low.
Target_Region: Auricular branch of vagus nerve (cymba concha)
Monitoring_Requirements: PHQ-9 weekly; HAMD; cardiac if indicated
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Ear discomfort; skin irritation; cough reflex
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician-guided parameter adjustment
Notes: GRADE evidence quality low to very low per 2023 meta — inform patients.

Protocol_Name: tDCS Anodal DLPFC for Multiple Sclerosis Fatigue
Evidence_Summary: IFCN 2017 Level B (PMID 27866120); Hanken 2016 (PMID 33196282) tDCS reduces MS fatigue.
Target_Region: Left DLPFC anode (F3); right supraorbital cathode (Fp2)
Monitoring_Requirements: MFIS/FSS; skin inspection
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review after 5 sessions
Notes: CON_ID mismatch: no ms-fatigue CON in conditions.csv — mapped to CON-017.

Protocol_Name: rTMS 10 Hz Left DLPFC for Schizophrenia Negative Symptoms
Evidence_Summary: IFCN 2017 Level B (PMID 27866120); Slotema 2014 (PMID 25034472).
Monitoring_Requirements: PANSS; SANS; adverse event log
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: Seizure; scalp discomfort; rare psychosis exacerbation
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review at 15 sessions
Notes: Condition updated to CON-023 Schizophrenia.

Protocol_Name: rTMS 10 Hz M1 for Spinal Cord Injury Central Pain
Evidence_Summary: IFCN 2017 Level B (PMID 27866120); Nardone 2017 meta-analysis (PMID 28332488).
Target_Region: Contralateral M1 (leg area for SCI lumbar; hand area if cervical)
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Figure-8 coil over M1 somatotopic area
Monitoring_Requirements: VAS/NRS; BPI; NPSI
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review at 10 sessions
Notes: Mapped to CON-008 chronic pain/fibromyalgia (closest CON).

Protocol_Name: tDCS Anodal Left IFG for Post-Stroke Aphasia
Evidence_Summary: IFCN 2017 Level B (PMID 27866120); Elsner 2019 Cochrane review (PMID 33197261).
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Anode over left IFG (F5/F7); cathode contralateral supraorbital
Monitoring_Requirements: WAB-R; Boston Naming; speech pathology assessment
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review after 10 sessions combined with speech therapy
Notes: Combined with speech therapy for best outcomes.

Protocol_Name: HRV Biofeedback for Generalized Anxiety Disorder
Evidence_Summary: Goessl 2017 meta-analysis (PMID 28264697, g=0.81); Lehrer 2014 (PMID 28286374).
Target_Region: Autonomic nervous system via HRV monitoring
Monitoring_Requirements: GAD-7; HRV metrics; stress diary
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Identify resonance frequency; progressive practice
Notes: Goessl 2017: g=0.81 for anxiety. Strong adjunctive evidence.

Protocol_Name: rTMS 1 Hz Right DLPFC for Bipolar Depression
Evidence_Summary: Slotema 2014 meta (PMID 25034472); Dell'Osso 2009 (PMID 19811529).
Monitoring_Requirements: MADRS; YMRS (monitor for mood switch)
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Stop if YMRS elevates; clinician review weekly
Notes: CON_ID mismatch: no bipolar-depression CON — mapped to CON-002 TRD.

Protocol_Name: PBM for Fibromyalgia (LLLT + Whole-Body)
Evidence_Summary: Yeh 2019 meta-analysis (PMID 31747571, 9 RCTs N=325, SMD=1.18 pain, 1.16 FIQ); Navarro-Ledesma 2022; Ribeiro 2023 (N=90).
Intensity: 25.2 J/cm² per site (Navarro-Ledesma); LLLT tender-point 6 J (de Carvalho)
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: LED/laser cluster whole-body + local tender points
Monitoring_Requirements: FIQ; VAS pain; PHQ-9
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review at 6 sessions
Notes: Yeh 2019 Grade B anchor.

Protocol_Name: tcVNS for PTSD (Cervical Vagus Nerve Stimulation)
Evidence_Summary: Bremner 2021 RCT (PMID 33262253, N=20 tcVNS, 3 mo twice daily, 31% greater PCL reduction); Gurel 2020 (N=25); Benzouak 2025 'very low certainty'.
Coil_or_Electrode_Placement: Gel-coated contact on neck over vagus
Monitoring_Requirements: PCL-5; IL-6; autonomic markers
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review at 4 weeks
Notes: Distinct from auricular taVNS. Grade C — very low certainty per Benzouak 2025.

Protocol_Name: HRV Biofeedback for PTSD
Evidence_Summary: Pyne 2019 WAR study (PMID 30020511, N=342, subgroup effect only in older soldiers d=-0.97 to -1.03); Schuman 2022 (PMID 36331685); Burch 2020 (PMID 32358782).
Monitoring_Requirements: PCL-5; PHQ-9; HRV metrics
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review at 6 sessions
Notes: Grade C due to subgroup-only main effect.

Protocol_Name: taVNS for Chronic Pain (Auricular)
Evidence_Summary: Costa 2024 meta (PMID 39131814, k=15 RCTs, ES 0.41 chronic pain; auricular subgroup ES=0.42).
Target_Region: Left cymba conchae (auricular) OR cervical tcVNS
Monitoring_Requirements: VAS; BPI; Pain phenotype stratification
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review at 4 weeks
Notes: Multiple verify flags — Costa 2024 pooled heterogeneous. Stratify by pain phenotype before clinical rollout.

Protocol_Name: EEG Neurofeedback (SMR/Beta) for Schizophrenia
Evidence_Summary: Duan 2025 meta-analysis (PMID 40225850, k=14, N=1371, SMD=-0.87 positive, SMD=-1.28 negative); Bauer 2025 (PMID 40886445); Shu 2025 (PMID 40445258).
Monitoring_Requirements: PANSS; SANS; cognitive testing
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review at 4 weeks; does NOT replace antipsychotics
Notes: Grade B meta-anchored (Duan 2025). Apify re-numbered from original PRO-034.

Protocol_Name: Transcranial PBM Helmet (635+810 nm) for Parkinson's Motor (Symbyx)
Evidence_Summary: Herkes 2023 RCT (PMID 38094162, N=40, SYMBYX helmet, 24 min/day x 6d/wk x 12 wk); McGee 2023 (PMID 37109183, ~70% responders); Battesha 2025 (PMID 41114920).
Monitoring_Requirements: MDS-UPDRS-III; functional motor tests
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review per institutional governance
Notes: Apify re-numbered from original PRO-035. Do NOT substitute for DBS in advanced PD.

Protocol_Name: Combined Transcranial + Intra-Oral PBM (904 nm) for Parkinson's (Pilot)
Evidence_Summary: Bullock-Saxton 2021 (PMID 34092640, N=22 triple-blind placebo); Liebert 2021 (PMID 34215216) proof-of-concept combined protocol.
Monitoring_Requirements: Spiral test; dynamic step test; 9-hole peg test
Adverse_Event_Monitoring: No AEs reported in Bullock-Saxton or Liebert
Escalation_or_Adjustment_Rules: Clinician review at 2 weeks
Notes: Apify re-numbered from original PRO-036. Pilot evidence only; requires dental clearance.
